name: AUSTIN LIGHT — Warm Editorial (final)
rationale: v1 read cheap because it stacked every wedding-template cliche: light optical Fraunces with italics (the "squiggly" the client named), a poetic headline crowded OVER children's faces, an amber-gold gradient on "make", film grain, a custom cursor, an oversized italic ampersand, and a logo cluster nobody could parse. Worst, the studio section led with a kissing-couple photo as if it were Edwin & Joyce. Expensive editorial earns its premium feel through restraint, scale, contrast, and hairline structure — not decoration. This inverts v1: warm PAPER but BLACK INK, one confident grotesk (Archivo) at huge sizes carrying 100% of the voice. Faint warm paper keeps the golden-hour brand warmth so it never feels corporate; the only color comes from the warm client photos — that cool-shell / warm-image tension is the Phaidon/Vogue monograph move that frames the work as art. One terracotta accent at under 3% coverage reads art-directed, not gimmicky. The split hero (calm type left, tall photo right) structurally removes all text from over faces, fixing the crowded-hero complaint. Honors every client note: bold sans, no serif, no gold gradient, split hero, clean Edwin & Joyce wordmark, client-work-only studio.

fonts.weights: Load BOTH from next/font/google. (1) Archivo — weights [400 body/UI, 500 labels+nav, 600 sub-heads/category names, 700 wordmark+links] subset latin display swap, expose as --font-sans. (2) Archivo Expanded (a separate, real Google Fonts family in the Archivo superfamily) — weights [700, 800] used ONLY for the hero name and the big section H2s to get the fashion-masthead WIDE stance; expose as --font-display. Body, nav, labels, and category H3s all use standard Archivo. Reserve Archivo Expanded strictly for display lines so the wide width reads as an art-directed masthead, not as everything-is-wide. Both are confirmed on Google Fonts and load cleanly via next/font/google. NO third family — the entire system is the Archivo superfamily so it reads as one deliberate typographic program, not a Frankenstein of mismatched fonts.
fonts.headingStyle: Big editorial display lines (hero name, section H2s like 'What we make', 'The two behind the lens', 'Let's tell your story') are SET IN SENTENCE CASE — large, tight, confident — NOT all-caps (the live Contact section already proves sentence-case grotesk reads premium here, and all-caps everywhere would feel like a generic agency masthead). Display tracking is NEGATIVE and tightens with size: -0.02em to -0.035em; line-height 0.92-0.95 so multi-line heads lock into a dense block. UPPERCASE is reserved strictly for small structural labels: nav items, eyebrows, category index numbers (01/02), stat captions, and meta — set at 0.7-0.75rem with POSITIVE tracking 0.14em-0.2em, weight 500. The corner wordmark 'Edwin & Joyce' is Archivo 700, sentence case, tracking -0.01em, with the ampersand in the SAME weight and size as the names (no italic, no oversize, no special treatment). NO italics anywhere — italics were the squiggly Fraunces problem. Numbers use tabular figures (font-feature-settings 'tnum'). Contrast comes from SIZE and WEIGHT, never from font-swapping.

typeScale: Push display BIG — that scale IS the design, and the jump from body to H2 should feel almost violent. Hero name 'Edwin & Joyce' (left panel masthead, can wrap to two lines): clamp(3.4rem, 9vw, 7.5rem), Archivo Expanded 800, tracking -0.03em, line-height 0.92 — the single biggest type on the screen. Section H2 ('What we make', 'The two behind the lens', 'Let's tell your story'): clamp(2.6rem, 6.5vw, 6rem), Archivo Expanded 700/800, sentence case, tracking -0.03em, line-height 0.95 — these dominate their screen. H3 / category names (Weddings, Couples & Engagements): clamp(1.7rem, 3vw, 2.6rem), Archivo 600, tracking -0.02em. Lede / hero supporting line: clamp(1.05rem, 1.6vw, 1.35rem), Archivo 400, line-height 1.5, color inkSoft. Body: 1rem-1.0625rem, Archivo 400, line-height 1.6, measure max 62ch. Eyebrows/labels: 0.7rem-0.75rem uppercase, weight 500, tracking 0.16em. Stat numerals (19+, 7, 2): go HUGE — clamp(3.5rem, 10vw, 8rem), Archivo Expanded 800, tabular-nums. Category index numerals (01-06): 0.75rem uppercase tabular with accent. Delete every .fluid-* serif class and the .display Fraunces rule; .display now = Archivo Expanded 800.

heroSpec.leftPanel: Left column on warm paper (#F4F0E8), generous air, lives inside a faint hairline grid. The wordmark 'Edwin & Joyce' lives in the fixed top nav and is NOT repeated here. Stack, top to bottom, left-aligned, the name+line+link group vertically centered with the eyebrow pinned near the top: (1) a small uppercase eyebrow — 'WEDDINGS · COUPLES · FAMILIES · PORTRAITS — AUSTIN, TX' in Archivo 500, tracking 0.18em, ~0.72rem, inkSoft, sitting above a thin hairline; (2) THE name as masthead 'Edwin & Joyce' in Archivo Expanded 800 at clamp(3.4rem,9vw,7.5rem), ink #15120E, tracking -0.03em, line-height 0.92 — the dominant element, ampersand same weight/size; (3) ONE short supporting line in sentence case (NOT a headline), lede size ~1.2rem Archivo 400 inkSoft, max 34ch: 'A husband-and-wife studio chasing the last warm light of the day.'; (4) a single 'View work →' action — NOT a pill button, a text link in Archivo 500 uppercase 0.78rem tracking 0.12em ink, the arrow in accent #C2562E, hairline underline that wipes left-to-right on hover and an arrow that nudges right. NO nav links, NO stacked Inquire button, NO region/years/scroll-cue clutter in this panel — keep it calm with lots of paper showing.
heroSpec.rightPhoto: Right column is a single tall full-bleed photo running floor-to-ceiling (100svh desktop) — one strong VERTICAL golden-hour CLIENT image (a backlit couple or senior at sunset fits the aspect best). Treatment: NO duotone, NO gold wash, NO heavy filter, NO text over it — the warm photo keeps its full natural color and is the ONLY color on the screen, which is the entire concept. object-fit cover, object-position center, no rounded corners. A single 1px ink hairline frames the left seam where photo meets the type panel. The ONLY optional adornment: a tiny vertical running-up caption on the photo's far-right margin — 'AUSTIN · TX' or a frame index 'F.01 — GOLDEN HOUR' in Archivo 500 uppercase tracking 0.2em, paper-colored, low opacity — placed over a dark region of the image OR given a barely-there bottom-edge scrim (rgba(20,17,13,0)->0.12) ONLY if legibility needs it. Use at most ONE vertical label site-wide. Single still hero — NO carousel, NO kenburns.
heroSpec.proportions: Desktop split 46% type / 54% photo via CSS grid (grid-template-columns: 46fr 54fr) — the photo gets the slightly larger share so the image leads visually like a magazine cover, while the type panel stays wide enough for the big name to breathe; at >=1600px allow 44/56. The dividing line is a single 1px ink hairline. MOBILE/tablet (<900px): STACK — photo FIRST as a tall full-bleed ~78svh block at top (image-first reads more confident on phones), then the type panel below on paper with eyebrow, name, supporting line, and 'View work →'. Name scales via clamp to ~clamp(2.8rem,12vw,4rem) but NEVER below ~2.5rem — it must still feel like a masthead. Never letterbox the photo on mobile; full-bleed width. Never overlay type on the photo at any breakpoint.
heroSpec.details: Structure via hairlines, not boxes: a 1px var(--line) rule under the top nav, the central 1px ink vertical seam splitting the panels, a thin rule under the eyebrow, and a bottom rule that becomes the seam to the next section — so the hero reads as a designed frame, not floating text. Scroll cue: replace the v1 animated glowing gold line with a tiny STATIC 'Scroll ↓' label or a single 12px tick in the bottom-left of the TYPE panel (inkSoft, uppercase tracking 0.22em) — quiet, not glowing. Contrast/legibility is AUTOMATIC because all hero TYPE sits on the paper panel, never on the photo (ink #15120E on #F4F0E8 is ~16:1; inkSoft #6B6155 is ~5:1 for the eyebrow/supporting line) — no text-shadow, no dark gradient over faces, and the 'no headline over faces' rule is satisfied structurally. The only text touching the image is the small vertical margin caption. Ensure the nav wordmark stays on the paper side (or flips to paper-color while over the photo region).

motion: RESTRAINED and editorial — motion should feel like a magazine spread settling, not a parallax demo or showreel. KEEP: (1) ONE masthead reveal on load — the hero name does a single clean clip/mask reveal (translateY up from a clipped mask, ~0.9s power3.out / cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1)); the supporting line + 'View work →' fade up after it (stagger ~0.08s). (2) Lenis smooth scroll (subtle, lerp ~0.08). (3) Section content fade-up on enter via the existing data-reveal/ScrollTrigger pattern — opacity 0->1 with a small 16-24px upward translate, ease cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), ~0.7-0.8s, fires ONCE, never stagger more than 3 items. (4) A VERY gentle scroll parallax on hero/section photos only (yPercent 6-8, scrub) — much softer than v1's 18%. (5) Quiet hairline underline wipe on links (~0.5s). (6) Subtle image scale 1.03 on work tiles on hover. (7) Stat numerals count up once on enter. CUT from v1: the 6s auto-advancing hero kenburns slideshow (one still photo, no kenburns scale); the glowing animated gradient scroll-line; the heavy hero-copy fade-to-0.2 on scroll; the magnetic custom cursor entirely (it fights accessibility and screams template — restore native pointer); the gold button fill-up sweep; any letter-by-letter / SplitText headline animation beyond the single masthead reveal (it cheapens big type — let scale do the work); bouncy springs and long parallax travel; the marquee if it loops gimmicky. Rule of thumb: nothing moves more than ~24px, nothing animates longer than ~0.9s, one effect per element. Honor prefers-reduced-motion fully (already wired) — everything resolves to its final state instantly.
remove: Fraunces serif and ALL serif type everywhere — replace with the Archivo / Archivo Expanded grotesk program; delete --font-display:var(--font-fraunces), the .display weight-350 + .display em italic rules, the .lede serif rule, and the Georgia/serif fallbacks (the 'squiggly' culprit)

Gold gradient system: --color-gold, --color-gold-bright, --color-clay, the .text-gold-grad class, the gold-fill .btn::before sweep, and the gold ::selection — replace with a single flat terracotta accent #C2562E used at <3% coverage; selection becomes accent or ink
The warm bone+amber palette tokens — collapse --color-paper/-deep/-soft and the gold trio down to paper/paperAlt/ink/inkSoft/line/night/accent
Film grain overlay (.grain::after SVG noise) — delete entirely; it muddies photos and screams 'cheap filter'
Custom magnetic cursor (Cursor.tsx + [data-cursor] hooks + the cursor-hiding CSS) — remove; restore the native OS pointer (also lets :focus-visible drop the gold outline for an ink/accent one)

The crowded hero serif headline 'Made of Light and the People You Love' OVER the children's photo, plus the .hero-shadow legibility hacks and the 86%-black bottom hero gradient — no text ever sits on the photo; type moves to the left paper panel
The hero auto-slideshow carousel + kenburns scale keyframes — single still hero photo

The confused logo cluster in Logo.tsx: drop 'ormeo', drop the 'ormeo · austin' subline, drop the long eyebrow, drop the oversized italic ampersand — wordmark is ONLY 'Edwin & Joyce' in Archivo 700 with a normal same-size ampersand

The 'the lens' studio photo of the kissing couple presented as the team — it implies a portrait of Edwin & Joyce; replace with an all-type spread (and optionally clearly-labeled client portfolio work), never a couple-portrait of the photographers
The italic pull-quote 'Honor the people, honor the light…' in serif italic and the gold accent on it — reset to upright Archivo, accent only on a trailing word if desired
The pill .btn rounded gold button — replace with a flat ink rectangle button (Inquire/Submit) and the underline text-link system; the glowing animated scroll-line — replace with a static tick
componentNotes: NAV (Nav.tsx): fixed, transparent over the hero then warm paper with a 1px bottom hairline on scroll. Left = wordmark 'Edwin & Joyce' (Logo.tsx simplified to Archivo 700, sentence case, tracking -0.01em, normal same-size ampersand, NO subline / NO ormeo / NO austin). Right = uppercase 500 links (Work · Studio · Experience · Contact, 0.75rem tracking 0.14em) + a single 'Inquire' that is a flat solid-ink rectangle button (no pill, no gold). While over the photo region the wordmark + links flip to paper color. WORK GRID (CategoryShowcase / SignatureGallery) — graft the strict editorial numbered grid: each of the 6 genres is one row led by a small index numeral '01'-'06' in tabular uppercase + accent, a big sentence-case category H3 (Archivo 600), a one-line ink-soft description, and a 'See gallery →' link with a terracotta arrow; large images alternate left/right across rows for rhythm with hairline dividers between rows, ~2px corners, generous whitespace, quiet scale-1.03 hover. The warm client photos are the only color. Replace the gold accent word in 'What we make' with plain ink (or accent only on the trailing word). STUDIO / ABOUT (Studio.tsx) — CRITICAL: remove the kissing-couple photo used as the team; never imply any photo is Edwin & Joyce. Rebuild as a confident all-TYPE editorial spread on the warm night bg (#14110D) for contrast: a small uppercase 'The Studio' label, a big sentence-case H2 + the 19-year husband-and-wife Austin story in a two-column body, a hairline-bordered fact list, and the 'Honor the people, honor the light' line as a large UPRIGHT Archivo statement (not serif italic). If images are shown they must be CLIENT work explicitly captioned 'Selected work / Recent sessions' so nothing reads as a portrait of the team. Keep the 'With love, Edwin & Joyce — Austin, Texas' sign-off in clean Archivo. STATS (StatsBand.tsx): pull OFF the photo background (no text over faces). Put 19+ / 7 / 2 at hero scale in Archivo Expanded 800 tabular-nums (clamp up to 8rem) on a clean paper or night band, separated by 1px vertical hairline rules, each with a small uppercase tracking-0.18em caption beneath, count-up on enter — the numbers ARE the graphic. CONTACT (Contact.tsx): keep the dark night section but recolor night to #14110D, retitle in Archivo Expanded sentence case 'Let's tell your story in the light', remove gold focus states (use a 1px paper or accent underline focus), inputs get bottom-hairline-only styling (no boxes), labels uppercase 0.7rem, submit is a flat accent or ink button. Show edwinormeo@gmail.com, 904-305-2732, @ejophotographyog plainly; FAQ rows stay hairline-divided. FOOTER (Footer.tsx): 'Edwin & Joyce' wordmark big in Archivo Expanded 800 on night, one short line ('Natural-light photography · Austin & the Texas Hill Country'), clean link columns + contact, a 1px top hairline, and replace the 'Crafted in the golden hour' twee gilt tagline with a plain '© 2026 Edwin & Joyce Ormeo Photography — Austin, Texas'. Drop any gold sitewide. GLOBAL: delete Cursor.tsx and the grain wrapper from layout; keep SmoothScroll (Lenis) and the GSAP reveal primitives.
whatChanged: Versus the rejected v1: (1) TYPE — the light, italic, "squiggly" Fraunces serif is gone entirely; one confident grotesk superfamily now carries everything (Archivo for body/UI/nav, Archivo Expanded 700/800 for the masthead-wide hero name and section H2s), set in sentence case at genuinely large editorial scale. (2) HERO — the poetic headline crowded OVER the children's faces is deleted; the hero becomes a hard 46/54 split with calm type on warm paper LEFT (eyebrow, big 'Edwin & Joyce' name, one supporting line, 'View work →') and a single tall full-bleed golden-hour client photo RIGHT, with zero text over faces; the 6s kenburns slideshow becomes one still image. (3) COLOR — the amber-gold GRADIENT, the gold button sweep, and the gold token trio are removed; palette is warm paper (#F4F0E8, brand warmth preserved) + near-black ink + one restrained terracotta accent at <3%, so the only real color on the page is the photography itself. (4) LOGO — the confusing cluster (Edwin & Joyce + 'ormeo · austin' subline + long eyebrow + oversized italic ampersand) collapses to a clean 'Edwin & Joyce' wordmark in Archivo 700 with a normal same-size ampersand. (5) STUDIO — the kissing-couple photo wrongly presented as Edwin & Joyce is removed; the section becomes a type-led spread that shows client work only, never implying a portrait of the team. (6) DECORATION — film grain, the custom magnetic cursor, the glowing animated scroll line, and heavy parallax are all cut; motion is reduced to one masthead reveal, gentle fade-ups, and quiet underline wipes. Net effect: it now photographs like a $15k brand — gallery wall, not brochure — while honoring every client directive (bold modern sans, split hero, clean wordmark, no fake portrait, no gold).
